schemas.description: Actions to lookup information from the web and answer user queries.

actions.description: The user request is a question about general knowledge that can be found from the internet. (e.g. "what is the current price of Microsoft stock?") look up for contemporary internet information including sports scores, news events, or current commerce offerings, use the lookups parameter to request a lookup of the information on the user's behalf; the assistant will generate a response based on the lookup results Lookup *facts* you don't know or if your facts are out of date. E.g. stock prices, time sensitive data, etc the search strings to look up on the user's behalf should be specific enough to return the correct information it is recommended to include the same entities as in the user request

parameters.name: originalRequest
parameters.description: the original request of the user

parameters.name: internetLookups
parameters.description: the internet search terms to use

parameters.name: sites
parameters.description: specific sites to look up in.

agents.name: calendar
agents.description: Agent integration with MS Graph's calendar

schemas.description: Calendar agent that keeps track of important dates and events. Use it to schedule appointments, set reminders, and organize activities.

actions.description: Add a participant to an event Examples: "add John to the meeting", "invite Sarah to lunch"

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Which event to add to (required) - plain string

parameters.name: participant
parameters.description: Who to add (required) - plain string

actions.description: Find events on the calendar Examples: "find my meetings today", "show meetings with Bob"

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: When to search (optional, defaults to future events) Use the user's EXACT words like "today", "tomorrow", "this week" - do NOT convert to ISO date

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: What type of event to find (optional, finds all if not specified) - plain string

parameters.name: participant
parameters.description: Find events with a specific participant (optional) - plain string

phrasings: what events do I have with {participant} on {date} about {description}
show me meetings about {description} scheduled for {date}
find all events on {date} that include {participant}

actions.description: Find all events happening this week Examples: "what do I have this week", "show my week"
phrasings: find what I have scheduled this week
show me all my events for this week starting {startDay}

actions.description: Find all events happening today Examples: "what do I have today", "show me today's schedule"
phrasings: what I have scheduled for today
show me all my appointments and meetings for {date}

actions.description: Remove an event from the calendar Examples: "delete my meeting", "remove the dentist appointment", "cancel lunch with Bob" Behavior based on number of matches: - 1 match: prompts for confirmation - 2-5 matches: prompts with list to select which event to delete - More than 5 matches: returns error asking user to be more specific

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: What event to remove (required) - plain string describing the event

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Optional date filter to narrow down which event to remove Use the user's EXACT words like "today", "tomorrow", "this week" - do NOT convert to ISO date

actions.description: Schedule a new event on the calendar Examples: "schedule a meeting tomorrow at 2pm", "add dentist appointment on Friday at 3pm"

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: What the event is about (required) - plain string, not an entity type

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: When the event occurs (required) - use the user's EXACT words like "today", "tomorrow", "Friday", "next week" Do NOT convert to ISO date format - the handler will parse temporal expressions

parameters.name: time
parameters.description: What time the event starts (optional, defaults to all-day if not specified) Use the user's EXACT words like "2pm", "3:30", "noon" - do NOT convert formats

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: Where the event takes place (optional) - plain string, not an entity type

parameters.name: participant
parameters.description: Who else should attend (optional, single participant) - plain string, not an entity type

phrasings: schedule a {description} for {date} at {time} in {location} with {participant}

agents.name: chat
agents.description: Agent for conversation and information lookup

schemas.description: Chat agent that helps you with general conversation and answering questions by looking up answers in past conversations or the internet.

actions.description: this is the way to handle requests for known information that is not stored in application memory or conversation memory, such as facts, definitions, explanations, captioning, or other information that can be generated without a lookup if the user request is a known phrase that is unrelated to the context, use this action to generate an explanation for the known phrase. this action is never used when the request is for private information from past conversations including private events, plans, projects in progress, and other items from discussions with team members or the assistant, unless the information is present in the chat history

parameters.name: originalRequest
parameters.description: the original request from the user

parameters.name: generatedText
parameters.description: the generated text to show the user; this should be a complete response to the user's request

parameters.name: userRequestEntities
parameters.description: ALL the actions and entities present in the text of the user's request including attachments

parameters.name: generatedTextEntities
parameters.description: ALL the actions and entities present in the generated text

parameters.name: relatedFiles
parameters.description: The file names of any attachments

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: file entities.

schemas.description: Code agent helps you with productivity in using an editor and performing actions like creating files, editor customization, writing code, intelligent code suggestions, real-time error detection, debugging, and source control tasks etc.

parameters.name: theme
parameters.type: "Default Light+" | "Default Dark+" | "Monokai" | "Monokai Dimmed" | "Solarized Dark" | "Solarized Light" | "Quiet Light" | "Red" | …
parameters.description: The VS Code color theme name, e.g. "Monokai", "Solarized Dark", "Dark+".

actions.description: Change the editor layout to single, double or three column layout

parameters.name: columnCount
parameters.description: e.g., "single", "double", "three" column editor layout. Fill only if specified by the user

actions.description: Launch or Start VSCode

Example: User: create a new typescript file called scratch.ts with a hello world function Agent: { actionName: "newCodeFile", parameters: { fileName: "scratch.ts", language: "typeScript", content: "function helloWorld() { console.log('Hello, World!'); }\n" } }

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: The file name; if omitted or empty, "untitled" is used.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: The programming language of the file.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Initial file content; empty string for an empty scratch file.

actions.description: Create a new Markdown (.md) file in the editor. Example: User: create a markdown file with a list of top ten AI papers Agent: { actionName: "newMarkdownFile", parameters: { fileName: "papers.md", content: "# Top 10 AI Papers\n..." } }

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: The file name; if omitted or empty, "untitled" is used.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Initial Markdown content; empty string for an empty scratch file.

parameters.name: fileName

actions.description: Split an editor window into multiple panes showing the same file or different files side-by-side. schemas.description: Code agent for in-editor refactor actions: insert/move cursor, fix code problems, generate code with Copilot, save current/all files.

parameters.name: docstring
parameters.description: A short natural language prompt or description of what the code block should do. Used for generating context-aware completions (e.g., via Copilot). Example: "Loop over x in descending order"

parameters.name: declaration
parameters.description: The starting line of a structured code block, such as a for-loop, if-statement, etc. Example: "for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {"

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Optional body of the code block, excluding the closing brace. Can be omitted if Copilot or the agent will generate it.

parameters.name: codeSnippet
parameters.description: A short or one-liner code expression to insert directly or use as a Copilot prompt. Example: "const total = prices.reduce(...)" If provided, takes precedence over declaration/body.

parameters.name: isPartial
parameters.description: True if the request is partial or uncertain (e.g., from speech). Used to guide how much the agent or Copilot should infer.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Optional target file where the code should be inserted. Only emit the file if specified by the user. If omitted, defaults to the active editor.

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: Position in the file where the code block should be inserted. Defaults to { type: "atCursor" }.

actions.description: @deprecated Use `NewCodeFileAction`, `NewMarkdownFileAction`, or `NewTextFileAction` instead.

parameters.name: folderName
parameters.description: Name of the folder to create the file in (e.g., "src")

parameters.name: folderRelativeTo
parameters.description: Optional: restrict to folders under this path or name

actions.description: Schema to generate a function

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Programming language of the function (determines syntax rules and formatting)

parameters.name: functionDeclaration
parameters.description: The full function declaration or signature line (e.g., "function foo(x: number): number {") This must include the opening brace (for JS/TS) or colon (for Python) and match the language's syntax

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Function implementation body (excluding the closing brace or dedent, if applicable) This should contain valid code that performs the intended task. If left as an empty string (""), the extension will attempt to trigger GitHub Copilot to complete the body. Prefer generating this value if the function behavior is well understood.

parameters.name: docstring
parameters.description: A one-line docstring or comment that explains what the function does. This should describe the function’s intent based on the declaration and user request. Used to improve readability and may help tools like Copilot complete the function more accurately.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Optional: the name of the function. If omitted, may be inferred from the declaration.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Optional: an array of function parameters with their names and types (for display or analysis)

parameters.name: returnType
parameters.description: Optional: the function's return type (e.g., "string", "void", "Promise<boolean>")

parameters.name: isAsync
parameters.description: Optional: whether the function should be marked as async

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Optional: the target file where the function should be inserted

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: Optional: where in the file to insert the function. Defaults to current cursor location if not provided.

actions.description: schema to fix a problem (diagnostic) in the code. The target can be the first problem, the next one, all, or a specific location/index in the file. Optional hints from the user (e.g. "fix the second error") or file scoping may guide the choice. Used when the user requests: "fix this problem", "fix the second error", "fix all issues in this file".

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Which problem to fix (e.g., "first", "next", "atCursor", or "second problem in file")

parameters.name: hint
parameters.description: Optional context hint from the agent (parsed from user request)

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: File scope (defaults to active editor)

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description: The generation strategy to use

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: Where to generate the code

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional language hint

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: Optional: natural language guidance (if not using inline)

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Optional: provide surrounding code or references

parameters.name: attemptLimit
parameters.description: Optional: how many suggestions to try (default: 1)

parameters.name: autoAccept
parameters.description: Optional: whether to auto-accept Copilot's first suggestion

parameters.name: explanationMode
parameters.description: Optional: let agent explain what Copilot might generate

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The comment content, excluding the comment syntax itself

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional, helps format comments (e.g., // vs #)

parameters.name: commentStyle
parameters.description: Comment style (default: "line")

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: Where to insert the comment

parameters.name: newlineBefore
parameters.description: Whether to add a blank line before the comment

parameters.name: newlineAfter
parameters.description: Whether to add a blank line after the comment

actions.description: Action for inserting or deleting lines in a file.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Operation to perform: "insert": add new empty lines at the target position "delete": remove lines starting from the target position

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of lines to insert or delete (default: 1).

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: Position where the operation should happen. Examples: { type: "atCursor" } { type: "afterLine", line: 10 } { type: "atEndOfFile" }

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Optional target file (defaults to the active editor).

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: When deleting: - true: allow deleting non-empty lines (default) - false: only delete if lines are empty/whitespace

actions.description: ACTION: Move the cursor to a specific position within a file (for navigation or editing preparation). This moves the cursor position, NOT split/duplicate the editor view. USE THIS for: "go to file X", "jump to line 50", "go to function foo", "move cursor to X" DO NOT USE for: "split editor", "split X", "duplicate editor" (use splitEditor action instead) Examples: - "go to line 50" → { target: { type: "onLine", line: 50 } } - "jump to function main" → { target: { type: "insideFunction", name: "main" } } - "go to app.tsx" → { target: { type: "inFile", filePath: "app.tsx" } } - "move cursor to the end of file" → { target: { type: "atEndOfFile" } }

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Target position for the cursor. Supports symbolic locations, line-based positions, or file-relative positions.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Optional file where the cursor should be moved. Defaults to the active editor if not provided.

parameters.name: hint
parameters.description: Optional user or agent hint to clarify ambiguous positioning. Example: "after function declaration", "before import statements".

schemas.description: Manage TypeAgent Shell conversations (chat tabs in the TypeAgent VS Code Shell extension): create a new conversation, rename the current conversation, or switch to an existing conversation by name.

actions.description: Delete a TypeAgent Shell conversation by display name. The current active conversation cannot be deleted. The extension will prompt the user for confirmation before deleting. Example: User: delete the "design review" conversation Agent: { actionName: "deleteConversation", parameters: { name: "design review" } } Example: User: delete a conversation Agent: { actionName: "deleteConversation", parameters: {} }

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The display name of the conversation to delete. If omitted, the user will be shown a picker in the extension UI.

actions.description: Create a brand-new TypeAgent Shell conversation (a new chat tab in the TypeAgent Shell VS Code extension) and switch the current tab to it. Example: User: start a new conversation Agent: { actionName: "newConversation", parameters: {} } Example: User: new conversation called "design review" Agent: { actionName: "newConversation", parameters: { name: "design review" } }

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Optional display name for the new conversation. If omitted, the user will be prompted in the extension UI.

actions.description: Rename the TypeAgent Shell conversation that is currently active in this chat tab. Example: User: rename this conversation to "vscode shell PR" Agent: { actionName: "renameConversation", parameters: { newName: "vscode shell PR" } }

parameters.name: newName
parameters.description: The new display name to apply to the current conversation.

actions.description: Switch the current TypeAgent Shell chat tab to a different existing conversation, identified by its display name. If no conversation with that name exists, a new one will be created with that name and the current tab will switch to it. Example: User: switch to the "design review" conversation Agent: { actionName: "switchConversation", parameters: { name: "design review" } } Example: User: switch conversation Agent: { actionName: "switchConversation", parameters: {} }

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The display name of the conversation to switch to. If omitted, the user will be shown a picker in the extension UI. If the named conversation does not exist, a new one will be created.

schemas.description: Desktop agent that allows you to manage the programs running on a computer, change Windows settings, control the desktop environment, and perform actions such as opening a file, closing a window, tiling windows, launching a program, maximizing windows, adjusting display settings, managing privacy settings, and controlling system features.

actions.description: Adjusts screen brightness (increase or decrease)
Adjusts system volume up or down by a relative amount

Apply a Windows desktop theme by name (e.g. "Captured Motion", "Glow", "Sunrise") or .theme file path. Affects the Windows wallpaper / sounds / cursors / window chrome. Example: User: change my windows theme to Glow Agent: { actionName: "ApplyTheme", parameters: { filePath: "Glow", themeName: "Glow" } }

parameters.name: filePath
parameters.description: The Windows theme name or .theme file path to apply (use "previous" to revert).

parameters.name: themeName
parameters.description: Optional theme name parroted by the LLM when the user mentions a specific theme; never read by the action — exists only to give the model a slot for the name so it doesn't squeeze a VS Code editor theme into `filePath`.

switches to the next Windows Desktop

Restarts a Windows service (stops it and starts it again). The target can be identified either by its service name / display name, or by a phrase found in the service's description. Example: User: restart the print spooler service Agent: { actionName: "RestartService", parameters: { service: "Print Spooler", matchBy: "name" } } Example: User: restart the service that manages windows updates Agent: { actionName: "RestartService", parameters: { service: "windows update", matchBy: "description" } }

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: The Windows service name or display name (e.g. "Spooler" or "Print Spooler") when matchBy is "name"; or a phrase to search for within service descriptions when matchBy is "description".

parameters.name: matchBy
parameters.description: How to locate the service. "name" (the default) matches the service name or display name; "description" searches each service's description text for the provided phrase.

parameters.name: elevate
parameters.description: Internal use only: set to true by the agent after the user has agreed to run the restart with administrator privileges. Never set this from the user's initial request — leave it unset so the agent asks for consent first.

actions.description: Change screen resolution
Changes the text size that appears throughout Windows and your apps

Use this for TypeAgent shell conversations only — NOT for media playlists, songs, files, or browser tabs.

actions.description: Delete a conversation by name. Use this when the user wants to remove, delete, or destroy a conversation. Examples: "delete conversation test", "remove the work conversation", "delete test2".

Create a new conversation and optionally give it a name. Use this when the user wants to create, start, make, or open a brand-new conversation. Examples: "create a new conversation", "start a new conversation called test", "make a new conversation", "new conversation named work", "open a new conversation test". IMPORTANT: use this only when the user is creating something new, not switching to an existing one.

Examples: "switch to next conversation", "next conversation", "go to the next conversation", "cycle to the next conversation".

Switch to the PREVIOUS TypeAgent conversation in the list (cycles around). Use this when the user wants to go to the previous TypeAgent shell conversation (NOT the previous song, previous track, or any other kind of "previous"). Examples: "switch to previous conversation", "previous conversation", "go to the previous conversation", "cycle to the previous conversation".

Rename a conversation. Use this when the user wants to rename, relabel, or give a new name to a conversation. If the user specifies which conversation to rename, capture it as 'name'. Examples: "rename this conversation to work", "rename test7 to test5", "call this conversation research", "rename current conversation to my project".

For "next"/"previous" without a specific name, use NextConversationAction or PrevConversationAction instead.
